lines.speaker: Grandmother Soel
lines.text: You remember how your father showed you. Small things first — tinder, breath, patience. The Mother does not reward rushing.

lines.speaker: Grandmother Soel
lines.text: Good. Let it catch. The smoke carries our gratitude up through the canopy. She hears it.

morality.event: Listened to Grandmother Soel at the fire

lines.speaker: Maret
lines.text: Morning. Don't take more than the bucket holds. The well has been quiet lately — Dovin thinks the eastern spring is shifting again.

lines.speaker: Maret
lines.text: He's probably right. He usually is about the water. Wrong about most everything else, mind you.

morality.event: Took only what was needed from the well

lines.speaker: Elder Voss
lines.text: Gather close. Today we give thanks for the fifty-third year of the Green Return. The soil remembers what was done to it. And it remembers what we chose to do differently.

lines.speaker: Elder Voss
lines.text: The Mother is not a name for something that lives above us. She is the name for what lives between us — between our hands and the ground, between our breath and the trees.

lines.speaker: Elder Voss
lines.text: Go gently today. The world is listening, as it always has been.
You have kind eyes. I've been watching this settlement for three days deciding whether to knock. Most places... you can tell by the smoke what kind of people live there.
Yours smells like pine and old bread. I decided to knock.

morality.event: Approached the stranger near the village edge
